heading: All-terrain vehicles
label: 2089 All-terrain vehicles

children.heading: Mandatory standard
children.label: 1 Mandatory standard
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, within 90 days after , the Commission shall publish in the Federal Register as a mandatory consumer product safety standard the American National Standard for Four Wheel All-Terrain Vehicles Equipment Configuration, and Performance Requirements developed by the Specialty Vehicle Institute of America (American National Standard ANSI/SVIA–1–2007). The standard shall take effect 150 days after it is published.August 14, 2008

children.label: A
children.text: the all-terrain vehicle complies with each applicable provision of the standard;

children.label: B
children.text: the ATV is subject to an ATV action plan filed with the Commission before , or subsequently filed with and approved by the Commission, and bears a label certifying such compliance and identifying the manufacturer, importer or private labeler and the ATV action plan to which it is subject; andAugust 14, 2008

children.label: C
children.text: the manufacturer or distributor is in compliance with all provisions of the applicable ATV action plan.

children.heading: Violation
children.label: 3 Violation
children.text: The failure to comply with any requirement of paragraph (2) shall be deemed to be a failure to comply with a consumer product safety standard under this chapter and subject to all of the penalties and remedies available under this chapter.

children.heading: Compliant models with additional features
children.label: 4 Compliant models with additional features
children.text: Paragraph (2) shall not be construed to prohibit the distribution in commerce of new all-terrain vehicles that comply with the requirements of that paragraph but also incorporate characteristics or components that are not covered by those requirements. Any such characteristics or components shall be subject to the requirements of .section 2064 of this title

children.heading: ANSI revisions
children.label: 1 ANSI revisions
children.text: If the American National Standard ANSI/SVIA–1–2007 is revised through the applicable consensus standards development process after the date on which the product safety standard for all-terrain vehicles is published in the Federal Register, the American National Standards Institute shall notify the Commission of the revision.

children.heading: Commission action
children.label: 2 Commission action
children.text: Within 120 days after it receives notice of such a revision by the American National Standards Institute, the Commission shall issue a notice of proposed rulemaking in accordance with to amend the product safety standard for all-terrain vehicles to include any such revision that the Commission determines is reasonably related to the safe performance of all-terrain vehicles, and notify the Institute of any provision it has determined not to be so related. The Commission shall promulgate an amendment to the standard for all-terrain vehicles within 180 days after the date on which the notice of proposed rulemaking for the amendment is published in the Federal Register.section 553 of title 5

children.heading: Unreasonable risk of injury
children.label: 3 Unreasonable risk of injury
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, the Commission may, pursuant to sections 2056 and 2058 of this title, amend the product safety standard for all-terrain vehicles to include any additional provision that the Commission determines is reasonably necessary to reduce an unreasonable risk of injury associated with the performance of all-terrain vehicles.

children.heading: Certain provisions not applicable
children.label: 4 Certain provisions not applicable
children.text: Sections 2056 and 2058 of this title shall not apply to promulgation of any amendment of the product safety standard under paragraph (2). Judicial review of any amendment of the standard under paragraph (2) shall be in accordance with chapter 7 of title 5.

children.heading: Requirements for 3-wheeled all-terrain vehicles
children.label: c Requirements for 3-wheeled all-terrain vehicles
children.text: Until a mandatory consumer product safety standard applicable to 3-wheeled all-terrain vehicles promulgated pursuant to this chapter is in effect, new 3-wheeled all-terrain vehicles may not be imported into or distributed in commerce in the United States. Any violation of this subsection shall be considered to be a violation of and may also be enforced under .section 2068(a)(1) of this titlesection 2066 of this title

children.heading: Deadline
children.label: 1 Deadline
children.text: The Commission shall issue a final rule in its proceeding entitled “Standards for All Terrain Vehicles and Ban of Three-wheeled All Terrain Vehicles”.

children.label: A
children.text: the weight of the ATV;

children.label: B
children.text: the maximum speed of the ATV;

children.label: C
children.text: the velocity at which an ATV of a given weight is traveling at the maximum speed of the ATV;

children.label: D
children.text: the age of children for whose operation the ATV is designed or who may reasonably be expected to operate the ATV; and

children.label: E
children.text: the average weight of children for whose operation the ATV is designed or who may reasonably be expected to operate the ATV.

children.label: A
children.text: suspension;

children.label: B
children.text: brake performance;

children.label: C
children.text: speed governors;

children.label: D
children.text: warning labels;

children.label: E
children.text: marketing; and

children.label: F
children.text: dynamic stability.

children.label: A
children.text: any motorized, off-highway vehicle designed to travel on 3 or 4 wheels, having a seat designed to be straddled by the operator and handlebars for steering control; but

children.label: B
children.text: does not include a prototype of a motorized, off-highway, all-terrain vehicle or other motorized, off-highway, all-terrain vehicle that is intended exclusively for research and development purposes unless the vehicle is offered for sale.

children.heading: ATV action plan
children.label: 2 ATV action plan
children.text: The term “ATV action plan” means a written plan or letter of undertaking that describes actions the manufacturer or distributor agrees to take to promote ATV safety, including rider training, dissemination of safety information, age recommendations, other policies governing marketing and sale of the ATVs, the monitoring of such sales, and other safety related measures, and that is substantially similar to the plans described under the heading “The Undertakings of the Companies in the Commission Notice” published in the Federal Register on (63 FR 48199–48204).September 9, 1998
